title: Kaiburr crystal
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Force artifact/Legends|Force relic]] from [[Mimban/Legends|Mimban]]|the [[cocktail]] of the [[Kaiburr Crystal|same name]]}} {{Artifact |image=[[File:HallaGazesAtCrystal.jpg]] |name=Kaiburr crystal |manufacturer= |model= |type=[[Crystal/Legends|Crystal]]''[[Splinter of the Mind's Eye]]'' |origin= |culture=[[Mimbanite]] |creators= |created= |destroyed= |discovered= |owners=*Priests of [[Pomojema]]''[[Rebellion Era Sourcebook]]'' *[[Meetra Surik]] {{C|Shard}}''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{C|Possibly}}In ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', a type of Lightsaber crystal is available in-game called the Kaiburr crystal. Where (and if) it appears is completely random, and it is possible for a player to miss it altogether. *[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]][[Jedi — The Dark Side 5|''Jedi — The Dark Side'' 5]] *[[Galen Marek]] {{C|Shard}}''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''The [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] shard is awarded in-game after the successful completion of a mission. However, as the game is dependent on giving the player the crystal based on performance in the level, its appearance is largely random. *[[Halla]] {{C|Shard, Crystal}} *[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] {{C|Shard, Crystal''[[Young Jedi Knights: Lightsabers]]''}} *[[Grammel]] {{C|Shard}} *[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] {{C|Shard}}{{GamerCite|5|The Emperor's Pawns}} *[[Lumiya]] {{C|Shard}} *[[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] {{C|Shard}}[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] |locations=*[[Temple of Pomojema]], [[Mimban/Legends|Circarpous V]] *[[Jedi Praxeum]], [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] {{C|Moved to}} |cost= |value= |shape= |size= |color= |material= |length= |width= |height= |weight= |protection= |capacity= |cont= |range= |inscription= |markings= |purpose=[[Force healing/Legends|Healing]] |heritage= |affiliation=[[Temple of Pomojema]] }} {{Quote|Although the Force exists everywhere and within all living things, certain artifacts have demonstrated the ability to augment it. One such artifact is the Kaiburr crystal of the planet Circarpous V, locally known as Mimban.|An excerpt of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi historian|Scholar]] [[Tionne Solusar]]'s writings|}} The '''Kaiburr crystal''' was an ancient [[The Force/Legends|Force]] relic, initially located on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mimban/Legends|Circarpous V]], locally known as Mimban. When in its natural location in the [[Temple of Pomojema|Temple of]] [[Pomojema]] on Mimban, the crystal increased a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive's]] power one thousand times over. Among its magnification of abilities were the [[Force healing/Legends|ability to heal]], to further augment a Force-user's connection to the Force, to project [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] by those typically unable, and to empower and energize a tired being close in proximity to the crystal. If cut properly, the Kaiburr crystal also worked as a [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystal]]. The Kaiburr crystal was initially used by priests to heal the sick in the Temple of Pomojema. However, for reasons unknown, the area was abandoned, and the crystal became a legend in the eyes of the local [[Mimbanite]]s. The crystal would go on as a legend until around [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], when an elder woman named [[Halla]] purchased from one of the Mimbanites a shard of the crystal, as well as a map which led to Pomojema's temple. The woman enlisted the help of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], on Mimban due to a crash-landing, and helped the two escape from [[Prison/Legends|prison]]. Unknown to the three of them, this caught the attention of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the]] [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]; unaware, she and her allies went on an expedition to acquire the ancient relic. They eventually reached the temple and found the crystal; however, Vader had beaten them there. Confronting Skywalker and Organa, Vader [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|engaged in a duel]] with both of them, eventually defeating Organa and driving Skywalker to the ground before losing his right arm to Skywalker's [[Anakin Skywalker's first lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and eventually losing the battle despite the power given to him by the crystal. Halla handed Skywalker the Kaiburr crystal, and, along with his inherent Force sensitivity, Luke used its legendary focusing powers to heal both himself and Organa, as the two were left critically injured after their duels. However, unbeknownst to any members of the party, the crystal lost its power as it left the proximity of the temple and was completely powerless once off-planet. The shard, which began the adventure on Mimban, would stay in Vader's possession until he gifted it to his apprentice, the Dark Lady [[Lumiya]]. Luke Skywalker later found use for the crystal off-planet, as a teaching tool when he started his [[Jedi Praxeum]] and as a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] focusing crystal. ==Properties== {{Quote|It feels warm… good.''"
"''You're crazy. It's a cold chunk of rock.''"
"''No, it's warm. Funny kind of warmth.|Luke Skywalker notes the properties of the Kaiburr crystal to Halla, shortly before falling into an unconscious healing trance|}} [[File:LukeTouchesShard.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Luke Skywalker touches the crystal shard, with interesting results.]] The Kaiburr crystal was a deep-crimson-colored crystal''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' which glowed with a faint inner light. Another variant was completely transparent, with no red coloration. When in the presence of a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] or in the [[Temple of Pomojema]], the crystal shone brighter. The crystal felt much like it looked: a cool, glowing piece of [[Glass/Legends|glass]]. However, when held by a Force-sensitive, as was the case of [[Halla]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], this feeling was enhanced by a sensation much like touching a live electrical current; this was due not to any inherent chemical properties of the crystal itself, but instead its connection to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. ===Effect on the Force=== {{Quote|Not… possible! Such power… in a child. Not possible!|Darth Vader reacts to Luke Skywalker's increased power|}} [[File:Kaiburr.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Luke Skywalker, suffused with the power of the Kaiburr crystal]] Proportionally according to size and density, the Force's power was magnified a thousandfold;''[[The Rise and Fall of Darth Vader]]'' while a single shard would give off a minor jolt, the entire crystal gave off such a feeling of power and clarity of the Force that the possessor's entire being would course with the energy. The full crystal was also able to channel the Force into a method of healing; Luke Skywalker used this inherent power, along with his limited connection to the Force, to bring both himself and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] back from the brink of [[Death/Legends|death]]. However, when [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] was in close proximity to the presence of the Kaiburr, the residual effects of the Force caused the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord's]] affinity with the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] to expand greater. As a result, Vader found himself losing what self-control he possessed, fighting his own son and—unbeknownst to him or Skywalker—his daughter against his will, as he had intended to capture them alive. Furthermore, the crystal granted Vader the ability to cast [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]], normally impossible due to Vader's bionic limbs and [[Darth Vader's armor/Legends|life-support systems]]. The Temple of Pomojema was the initial resting place of the crystal, where it was used by priests under [[Pomojema]] to heal the sick through the Force. However, the crystal's abilities, whether the full crystal or a mere shard, decreased substantially in direct relation with distance from Pomojema's temple.''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]'' This meant any metaphysical properties held by the Kaiburr crystal became less and less powerful, and by the time the crystal made it off-[[Planet/Legends|planet]], it was essentially worthless, save for its history and use as a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|focusing crystal]]. ===Use in a lightsaber=== [[File:Lumiya_Kaiburr.jpg|thumb|left|170px|Lumiya seizing the Kaiburr crystal.]] {{Quote|The most powerful and sought-after gems are rare Kaiburr crystals. However, though lightsabers are powerful weapons, their design is so flexible that practically any kind of crystal can be used. And, since I don't happen to have a stash of Kaiburr crystals, you'll have to make do with something else, of your own choosing.|Excerpt from a presentation given by Luke Skywalker on lightsaber creation|}} Shards could be removed from the main crystal easily; as such, these crystal fragments could be utilized as focusing crystals in lightsaber-type devices, including variants such as the [[Lightwhip/Legends|lightwhip]] or [[Shoto lightsaber/Legends|shoto]]. In fact, the crystal's properties made it, according to Luke Skywalker, the most powerful type of lightsaber crystal, and also one of the most sought-after. Later, Skywalker, his sister Leia Organa Solo, and several other [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in the [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] also used these shards for their lightsabers. Dark Lady of the Sith [[Lumiya]] would also utilize the crystal in her personal lightwhip;{{DB|character|lumiya|Lumiya}} the fragmented piece she utilized was the same piece that had belonged to Halla, confiscated by Vader long before. {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords}} [[Meetra Surik]] utilized one such fragment, described mysteriously only as a very powerful crystal for use in a lightsaber. {{Endgame}} {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Force Unleashed|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' video game}} Darth Vader's secret [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Galen Marek]], was awarded the shard of the clear variant of the Kaiburr crystal after a successful mission. Marek was then able to utilize the crystal as a focusing crystal; the natural properties of the Kaiburr shard the apprentice used helped augment his [[Telekinesis/Legends|ability to push]] through the Force. {{Endgame}} ==History== ===Creation, legend, and use in the galaxy=== {{Quote|Remember the old legends… how the temple priests could heal?|Halla reminds Luke Skywalker of the power of the crystal|}} [[File:PomojemaStatue.jpg|thumb|right|210px|The statue of [[Pomojema]].]] The crystal was known to have existed as early as [[3951 BBY]]. Due to its mysterious nature, several myths were formulated to explain the origins and usage of the Kaiburr crystal.[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 138|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 138]] One was formulated by the residents of [[Mimban/Legends|Circarpous V]], a planet locally known as Mimban, who began attaching the crystal to one of their legends. These legends stated that the crystal was created by Pomojema, a third-rate local god described and known as the god of the Kaiburr. In his temple, his priests would bolster their healing powers to aid the sick with the Kaiburr crystal. Certain elements of this particular myth were shown to be true by Luke Skywalker; however, as it was also later proven, the crystal was only the agent for healing, as it was instead the Force, acting through the priests, which was healing Pomojema's patients. The crystal was held by a stone statue of Pomojema inside the large temple ziggurat, and guarded by a [[hssiss]]. {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords}} Beyond the localized use, a fragment of the Kaiburr crystal was found off of Mimban. As it was during the time of the Jedi Exile [[Meetra Surik]] in 3951 BBY, she acquired a shard of the powerful crystal, and utilized it in her lightsaber as a focusing crystal. {{Endgame}} Multiple millennia later, by the time of the waning years of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] had obtained at least one Kaiburr crystal shard. In [[53 BBY/Legends|53 BBY]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] lost his lightsaber while carrying out a [[Telosian Civil War|mission]] on the planet [[Telos IV]], and after he had departed from Telos, he contacted the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] to inform them of the outcome of the mission. After receiving confirmation that Jinn had lost his lightsaber, Jedi Master [[Micah Giiett]] stated that a Kaiburr crystal would be made available to Jinn, so that he could use it in the construction of a replacement lightsaber. {{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Force Unleashed|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' video game}} About seventy years afterward, Darth Vader's secret apprentice came into possession of a shard of the crystal. The alternately-colored shard was given to the apprentice after the successful completion of one of his missions to confront rogue Jedi. Marek would then utilize the crystal inside his lightsaber to boost his connection to the Force. {{Endgame}} ===The shard=== {{Dialogue|Leia Organa|This Kaiburr crystal, just what is it supposed to be… a big gemstone of some kind?|Halla|Of some kind. Interested in spite of yourself, ain't you?|Luke Skywalker|We're interested in anything that brings us closer to getting off here. I have to say this story of the crystal sounds intriguing on its own. What kind of gem is it?"''
'''Halla''': ''"Pfagh! I could care less what kind of necklace it could make for some spoiled noblewoman, boy. I'm more interested in a certain property it's supposed to have.|attr=Halla reveals to Luke Skywalker and Leia Organa her goal|src=}} [[File:KaiburrShardBox.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Halla dumps the shard out of its box.]]Aside from very isolated appearances, the crystal was mainly unknown by [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], known only to the citizens of Circarpous V as a local myth. Halla, an elderly Force-sensitive woman who claimed to be a Jedi Master, knew of the legend and the reports of [[Archaeology/Legends|xenoarchaeologists]] concerning the crystal. She determined discovered such material after purchasing a remnant of the crystal from a local in exchange for an [[Alcohol/Legends|alcoholic]] beverage as well as through studying the crystal. Halla approached Luke Skywalker and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] Leia Organa, stranded on Mimban while en route to a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] meeting on the nearby world of [[Circarpous X]], due to a crash-landing forced by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Mining/Legends|mining]] operations taking place on the surface, and revealed her sliver of the Kaiburr crystal, which she held in a small box for safekeeping. After explaining the supposed properties of the crystal, Halla offered the two stranded Rebels a chance to gain passage off-world in exchange for assisting her in finding the crystal. Unfortunately for the older woman, Organa remained skeptical throughout the demonstration; it was not until Halla offered Skywalker the shard to hold, so he could feel its power, that she agreed to the expedition. However, due to a fight they caused, the two Rebels were detained by [[Captain-Supervisor]] [[Grammel]] and held in a converted Mimban temple which served as the Imperial headquarters. Skywalker still possessed the crystal fragment; Halla and the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] had escaped detection and were in hiding. Once at the Imperial headquarters, Grammel interrogated the two; Skywalker attempted to hide the nature of the visit to the [[Circarpous system/Legends|Circarpous Major system]] and was moderately successful. However, Grammel did recognize the two as the same pair who had met with Halla, known locally for her stories and frowned upon by the Imperial presence. Grammel requested that Skywalker hand over the box containing the crystal shard. After a brief attempt at escape by Organa, Skywalker was forced to hand over the still-open container, and the two were placed in the maximum security cell, shared by two [[Yuzzem]], located within the [[Prison/Legends|prison]]. ====Imperial entanglements==== {{Quote|Here it is.''"
"''Strange… I've never seen anything like that, Grammel. The radiation is generated from within?''"
"''Yes, sir, I'm certain.''"
"''I'm not, but I admit it looks to be so.|Grammel and Essada discuss the confiscated crystal shard|}} [[File:GrammelShard.jpg|thumb|right|220px|[[Grammel]] contemplates the shard.]] While Grammel contemplated the crystal fragment, he contacted local [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Bin Essada/Legends|Bin Essada]]. Essada in turn contacted [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader, because the governor recognized the young [[Gender/Legends|female]] prisoner as Princess Leia Organa of the recently destroyed planet [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. Meanwhile, the Yuzzem, who identified themselves as [[Hin]] and [[Kee (Yuzzem)|Kee]], took a liking to their new roommates. Skywalker was able to communicate in the [[Yuzz]]em tongue, thereby allowing himself and Organa to learn of the two's [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] and service to the Empire. The four eventually escaped, with the timely help of Halla, C-3PO, and R2-D2. When Halla learned that Grammel had indeed captured the fragment, she was unmoved, convinced that the group would soon possess the actual crystal. After the Rebels' escape and following destruction of the facility with [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]] thrown by Skywalker, Grammel, who had been injured and later treated from the blasts, met with Vader, who confiscated the crystal piece as retribution for Grammel's failure to keep the prisoners confined. When the Dark Lord came into contact with the shard, he decided he wanted to track down the relic in addition to catching his son and the Princess. ====Journey to the Temple==== {{Quote|Pomojema, god of the Kaiburr. It almost seems familiar, somehow. That's crazy, of course.|Halla spots the statue of Pomojema|}} [[File:TempleOfPomojema.jpg|thumb|left|220px|The [[Temple of Pomojema]].]] Skywalker, Organa, Halla, the two Yuzzem, and the two droids were able to escape Grammel and left the [[City/Legends|city]] in a commandeered crawler, with Vader following close behind. The were eventually forced to detour to a subterranean route to the temple, where they met the nomadic [[Coway]], whom they befriended after a slight incident between Skywalker and one of the Coway warriors. The two groups allied together to battle and defeat an Imperial force. Both teams soon after the slight interruption resumed their journey for the temple. The Imperial expedition would reach the temple first, allowing Vader plenty of time to organize a plan to capture Skywalker and the crystal. Unfortunately for Skywalker, due to the proximity to the crystal, he was unable to sense either Vader's or the rest of the Imperial team's presence. While examining the temple, Halla located the statue of Pomojema, which held the crystal in its stony grasp. Before they could grab it, an immense hssiss appeared out of the shadows and began an attack. Skywalker held the creature back as Halla stood transfixed by the crystal, which had consumed much of her time. In the midst of the battle with the hssiss, though, Skywalker sliced through one of the support beams of the temple, toppling the roof on both himself and the hssiss. While the collapse killed the beast, it also hindered Skywalker's movement, as his right leg was pinned underneath the fallen stone. ====Duel with a Dark Lord==== {{Quote|I'm going to kill you, Darth Vader.''"
"''What a high opinion you hold of yourself, Skywalker.''"
"''I'm… I'm Ben Kenobi.''"
"''Ben Kenobi's dead. I killed him myself. You are simple Luke Skywalker, an ex-farm boy from Tatooine. You are no master of the Force and the equal of Ben Kenobi you will never be.''"
"''Ben Kenobi is with me, Vader, and the Force is with me too.|Luke Skywalker confronts Darth Vader while infused with the Force by Obi-Wan Kenobi due to the latter's proximity to the Kaiburr crystal|}} [[File:Leia_fighting_Vader_on_Mimban_EGF.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Leia dueling Vader for control of the Kaiburr crystal.]] At this time, Vader appeared in the doorway, and began to taunt both Skywalker and Organa. Vader noticed the effects of his proximity to the Kaiburr crystal just before he engaged in a duel with Organa. Little by little he realized that the self-control he had was slowly fading away. His earlier intention to apprehend the two Rebels was soon overcome with a desire to kill them. Leia dealt Vader a few blows, but was soon bested due to the [[Sith/Legends|Sith's]] skills. Before her defeat, however, she handed off the saber to Skywalker, freed from the rubble by Hin. Skywalker stood before Vader, with his saber brandished in front of him, and engaged in combat with the Dark Lord. At this point, the [[Force ghost/Legends|spirit]] of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], Skywalker's former mentor, entered and began fighting through Skywalker.[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 19|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 19]] After a bit of time, Vader grew exhausted; however, a boost of energy drawn from the Kaiburr crystal refreshed him. Also resulting from his proximity to the crystal, Vader was able to project Force lightning, a feat thought impossible due to his artificial limbs.An explanation was given by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] in [[Star Wars Insider 80|''Star Wars Insider'' 80]], prior to the release of ''Rise and Fall of Darth Vader'', which explained that the "Force lightning" that Vader projected wasn't truly lightning, but rather [[Kinetite/Legends|Kinetite]]. However, the newer book, ''Rise and Fall'', retained the thought that Vader projected Force lightning; no explanation has been given as a definitive answer. Much to his surprise, though, Skywalker managed to block the oncoming bolts, and was able to fight again. Skywalker soon gained the upper hand when he cut through Vader's right arm. Vader calmly removed the still-lit saber from his disembodied hand and advanced to fight again, but tripped over his right arm into a deep pit. Vader would eventually exit the pit, but not before realizing just how detrimental the crystal was to his mission; while it increased his power, it also overrode his willpower. ====The duel's end==== {{Quote|It was the crystal, Leia. It healed me, it healed you, and I wasn't even aware of it doing so. Everything Halla surmised about it is true. It ''does'' use the Force. The crystal healed you, Leia… not me."''
''"Now, Luke boy. You were the agent the crystal worked through. Without you, wouldn't be nothin' but rock|Luke Skywalker's explanation|}} [[File:LukeHealsLeia.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Luke heals Leia with the Kaiburr crystal.]] With the Dark Lord out of the picture, Halla approached the two fallen Rebels, exhausted from the battle: Organa was afflicted with several burns and cuts, and Skywalker himself was close to death. Halla told him that she was a mere charlatan of the Force, not a master, and that she could do nothing with the crystal; after revealing this information, she handed the crystal to the young Jedi. Skywalker had a differing opinion—when he held the crystal, it felt warm to him, much to the surprise of the elder woman. Enveloped in an odd [[Color/Legends|red]] light, Skywalker first went into a healing trance to fix his own injuries, then moved to Organa's side, and, with the crystal in his hands, traced over her injuries, healing them. The entire healing process took only a few seconds. She awoke shortly after, free of injury beyond a simple [[Headache/Legends|headache]]. The group left the temple, crystal in hand, and decided a way to get off of Mimban without notifying the Imperial presence. ===Later use=== {{Quote|I must say that my new lightsaber is a significant improvement on my old one. But more than that, I feel more connected to this weapon because it carries a part of my history. A long time ago, Luke and I were saved by the Kaiburr crystal. Although I am prepared to activate my lightsaber in the line of duty, it is my hope to employ the crystal so that it continues to save lives, not take them.|Leia Organa Solo, in a recording made as part of her induction into Jedi Knighthood|}} Ultimately, the crystal proved to lose its power directly proportional to its distance from the temple.[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] Skywalker nevertheless retained the crystal, as returning it to Circarpous V would make it a target for thieves or the Empire, and later used it as a teaching tool for his students at the Jedi Praxeum on Yavin 4. Skywalker also performed some tests on the crystal, and found that, if cut properly, its shards could act as powerful focusing crystals. In a speech given to several Jedi in his Praxeum, Skywalker stated that while shards of the Kaiburr crystal were the most powerful and desired lightsaber focusing crystals, he did not possess any at the time. Later, however, he and some of his students would utilize fragments of the crystal for personal use in their lightsabers. Leia Organa Solo, after nearly forgetting about the crystal until [[35 ABY/Legends|thirty-three years]] after her adventure on Mimban, used a fragment as a focusing crystal, though she hoped that her use would save, not harm or take lives. Dark Lady of the Sith Lumiya, an Imperial agent, infiltrator, [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]], and apprentice to Darth Vader, also utilized a Kaiburr crystal sliver for use in her lightwhip. The piece she used was the same as the one Halla possessed and that Vader had acquired. Lumiya's master gifted the crystal to her. Later, during the time of the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Alema Rar]] infiltrated the Dark Lady's sanctuary, where she discovered a crate of Kaiburr crystal shards. In her searches, Rar dumped the crate and left it scattered on the floor for Lumiya to find when she returned.''[[Legacy of the Force: Inferno]]'' Lumiya would continue to use the crystal shard in her lightwhip until her death at Luke Skywalker's hands in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]].''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]'' ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|The Force is all around us. It can be collected and transmitted through the use of a Kiber Crystal. It's the only way to amplify the power of the Force within you.|Ben Kenobi, in "The Star Wars: From the Adventures of Luke Starkiller, Third-Draft, August 1975," an early draft of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]|''[[The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film]]''}} [[File:SOTME1978-ART.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Vader, with the crystal beneath him, faces Skywalker and Organa.]] The idea of the Kaiburr crystal can be traced to the earliest incarnations of the script for [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']]; specifically, the [[January]], [[1975]] "Story Synopsis and Typed Outline" from "[[Adventures of the Starkiller, Episode I: The Star Wars|The Adventures of Luke Starkiller (episode one) 'The Star Wars']]." There it was spelled as a "Kiber Crystal" and acted as a [[Wikipedia:MacGuffin|MacGuffin]], or device driving the story, for the heroes to capture. Ben Kenobi was intended to explain to Luke Skywalker how the Kiber Crystal collected the Force and allowed the wielder to use it.{{DB|technology|kaiburrcrystal|Kaiburr crystal}} Eventually, in a later edition of the draft, ''[[The Star Wars: From The Adventures of Luke Starkiller]], Third-Draft, [[August]] 1975,'' the plot was expanded to include Kenobi attempting to capture his personal Kiber Crystal from the "Crystal Chamber" on the Imperial throne world of Alderaan. The elder Jedi would explain how it had been taken from him during the Battle of Condawn—the same battle in which Skywalker's father died—by one of Kenobi's disciples. The few crystals that remained were held in the Alderaanian Crystal Chamber. Later, Skywalker was to utilize the crystal in his fight with Darth Vader. The original drafts placed the budget for the crystal at £300 (approximately $592). The idea was ultimately dropped, however, when [[George Lucas]] decided not to use a talisman such as this crystal and instead let the Force be used without an external source, feeling that the crystal detracted too much from the saga of Skywalker and Vader. The [[Star Wars Legends]] novel ''[[Splinter of the Mind's Eye]]'' reused this idea of such a crystal. The name "Kiber" was eventually reused in scripts for [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], where it was to used in the name of the dart that killed [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]]. Originally called a "Kyberdart," the dart's name was changed to become the [[Kamino/Legends|Kaminoan]] [[Mk I Saberdart|saberdart]].{{DB|technology|kaminosaberdart|Kamino saberdart}} The [[Kyber dart]] was eventually reused in the video game ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', as one of [[Mira]]'s armaments for her personal wrist-mounted [[Missile launcher/Legends|rocket launcher]]. The name and concept of a Force-attuned crystal would later be used as the [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|kyber crystal]], first featured in the television series ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' episode [[The Gathering (episode)|The Gathering]], aired in early [[November]] [[2012]],{{TCW|The Gathering (episode)}} named in [[A Test of Strength|the next episode]],{{TCW|A Test of Strength}} and featured throughout the series' sixth season.''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' With the advent of the new [[canon]] ''[[Star Wars]]'' stories beginning in [[April]] [[2014]], the name "kyber crystal" would eventually come to describe all crystals used in the construction and use of a lightsaber.{{SWE|technology|lightsaber-crystals|lightsaber crystals}} In the [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (video game)|''Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith'' video game]], "KAIBURR" is used as a cheat code. When activated, the player's character would be granted unlimited mastery of the Force in a clear connection to the crystal's properties.[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (video game)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' video game]] The Kaiburr crystal also appears in ''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'' as a fragment of the main crystal, where it is one of the most powerful focusing crystals one can install in a lightsaber or shoto. In the [[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' video game]], the crystal makes an appearance. Its physical attributes are different than every previous depiction; however, its use as a lightsaber focusing crystal, as well as its use to boost the user's Force abilities, remain intact.
